task_title: Lane_01 CTO review of 2 pending packets (Boot Minimum + Token/Context Protocol)
phase: Communication Layer Bootstrap (post-canon-activation review of remaining Lane_03 drafts)
scope: Review 2 packets: (1) Boot Minimum V1 draft at CANON/AIER_CODE_BOOT_MINIMUM_V1_draft.md; (2) Token/Context Protocol V1 packet AMD_LANE03_AIER_CODE_TOKEN_CONTEXT_PROTOCOL_2026-04-26 with target file at CANON/aier_code_token_and_context_optimization_protocol_v_1_draft.md.

files_NOT_touched.draft_files: Both packet draft files (Boot Minimum + Token Protocol) untouched (review-only)

PACKET_1_BOOT_MINIMUM.summary: Solid concise boot context (114 lines, ~1100 tokens). Compatible with active Memory Spec §15.2 cold-start protocol (subset). Strong NTS authority + 12-item forbidden actions list. Cross-references to real active files (SHARED_INDEX, LAW_SYSTEM, REDLINES, runtime/current_state, MASTER_CHECKLIST). Ready for NTS approval/apply with 3 non-blocking notes.

PACKET_2_TOKEN_PROTOCOL.summary: Substantive 914-line protocol spec defining 5-layer context loading model (Boot Minimum / Task Context / Document Stack Index / Summarized State / Future RAG). Strong authority chain (GitHub > indexed db > RAG > chat). Genuinely solves R-MEM-01 token budgeting via §16. Compatible with Path B authenticated API. Ready for NTS approval/apply with 5 non-blocking notes (most importantly: §7.2 + §20 path recommendations need update post-canon-activation; §8.3 + §14.3 current_state schema list matches Memory Spec Tier B not shipped Tier A).

PACKET_2_TOKEN_PROTOCOL.coherence: PASS — strong internal logic, 5 layers with purpose/requirements/redlines, clean flow
PACKET_2_TOKEN_PROTOCOL.authority_safety: PASS — §0 not-active-until-NTS; §22 redlines include no-chat-as-truth, no-RAG-as-authority; §9.4 RAG redline; explicit authority chain (GitHub > indexed db > RAG > chat)
PACKET_2_TOKEN_PROTOCOL.alignment_with_active_v0_canon: MOSTLY PASS — §5.2 boot files matches Boot Minimum; §6 task context aligns with patterns; §7/§8/§14 reference current_state; §13 task context template aligned. One issue: §8.3 + §14.3 current_state required-fields list matches Memory Spec v0.2 Tier B (future formal target) NOT shipped Tier A (the actual current_state.md schema)
PACKET_2_TOKEN_PROTOCOL.token_protocol_specific_compliance: PASS — §16 explicit context budgeting per task type; §22 redlines preserve authority chain; §17 properly gates full-repo review; compatible with Path B authenticated API model
non_blocking_notes: §7.2 + §20 path recommendations written before canon activation — say 'Until NTS decides otherwise, recommended path is docs/LAW_CLA_LLM/CANON/' but NTS has now decided (via T-L01-NTS-APPROVE-AND-MIGRATE-CANON-STACK-001 commit 8646dba) that canon stack lives at SHARED/architecture/. Protocol should reflect this. Recommend final home for the protocol itself: SHARED/os_operations/ (it's a procedural spec) OR colocate at SHARED/architecture/.

§8.3 + §14.3 current_state schema list matches Memory Spec v0.2 Tier B (future formal target deferred to T-RUNTIME-002) NOT the shipped Tier A (the actual file). Recommend Tier-aware language: 'For current shipped state, see Memory Spec §6.3 Tier A; for future formal target, see §6.4 Tier B (T-RUNTIME-002)'.
Hard-coded canonical path D:\UZG\Projects-v2\Uniton_Shared (carry-over issue across Lane_03 drafts).
§5.2 lists 6 boot files vs Boot Minimum §4 lists 7 items — minor consistency note; should align exactly.

Document length 914 lines is heavy for a 'don't read everything' protocol (slightly ironic). Could be split into Boot Minimum (already separate Packet 1), Task Context Template (separate task per §13), Document Stack Index (separate task per §7), with the core protocol shorter. Acceptable as one-time umbrella read; not blocking.

answers_to_lane_03_6_review_questions.Q1_5_layer_safe: YES — well-defined layers each with purpose/redlines; Layer 5 RAG properly gated as 'helper not authority'
answers_to_lane_03_6_review_questions.Q2_BOOT_STATE_CONTEXT_TARGET_sufficient: YES for normal tasks; §17 properly identifies when full-repo review is allowed (audit, crisis, migration, security, release readiness)
answers_to_lane_03_6_review_questions.Q3_CANON_or_SHARED_boot_or_SHARED_os_ops: Now that canon stack is at SHARED/architecture/, recommend SHARED/os_operations/ for this procedural spec, OR colocate at SHARED/architecture/. Do NOT leave in CANON/ (which is being phased out as a canon home post-activation).
answers_to_lane_03_6_review_questions.Q4_conflicts_with_Memory_Communication_O: Memory Spec: §8.3/§14.3 conflict with shipped Tier A schema (note 2 above). Communication Spec: aligned. OS Operations Spec: aligned. Roadmap Spec: aligned. ONE conflict identified, fixable via Tier-aware wording.
answers_to_lane_03_6_review_questions.Q5_estimates_keep_or_soften: KEEP as planning estimates — the doc itself already qualifies them as 'planning estimates, not measured guarantees' which is appropriate softening
answers_to_lane_03_6_review_questions.Q6_split_into_Boot_TaskContext_DocStackI: Boot Minimum is ALREADY drafted separately (Packet 1 in this review). Task Context Template and Document Stack Index can be subsequent draft tasks (§21 Implementation Plan already lists these as follow-up tasks). Token Protocol itself stands as the umbrella spec. NO need to split the umbrella.
